IF PLAINTIFF HAS NOT FILED A DISMISSAL BY THE TIME OF THE HEARING, THE JUDGE PRO TEM WILL WANT THE PARTIES TO ADDRESS WHY THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THE RULING OF THE COURT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1308(A)(1). Notice Of And Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Deft Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, Mcmahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
Off Calendar Jan-02-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF DIANA ANDERSON's Motion Fpr Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, Mcmahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process IS Off calendar. Plaintiff filed a Request for Dismissal on entire action on December 29, 2014. JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of And Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Deft Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, Mcmahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
Off Calendar Dec-22-2014 Continued To Jan-02-15 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on Monday, December 22, 2014. The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff DIANA ANDERSON'S Motion Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Victoria Brenner, And Monetary Sanctions Against Her And Her Attorneys Christina Kim And Gilsleider, McMahon & Hermanson For Misuse Of The Discovery Process is continued to January 2, 2015 at 10:00AM telephonic hearing before Judge Pro Tem Anthony Schoenberg. The JPT provides, by email, the dial-in information to the parties. If Plaintiff has not filed a dismissal by the time of the hearing, the Judge Pro Tem will want the parties to address why the tentative ruling is not, as a matter of law, the ruling of the Court pursuant to California Rule of Court 3.1308(a)(1). Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
